The '''Airam''' was a clan-based culture of indeterminate [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] within the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

==History==
At least one Airam facility was located in the [[The Drift/Legends|Kuras Drift]] in the adjacent [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]].

The Airam were considered fiercely independent. Known clan leaders were [[Tamaron]] and [[Ilay]].
The appearance they gave of being a less-than law-abiding people was not helped by their attachment to occasional [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]] and other illicit activities.
The Airam were also competent technicians. Members of one clan aided the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in its project to transfer [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity-well generator]] technology from a captured [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor cruiser]] to an Alliance [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class cruiser]].

Suicidal fanaticism was not unknown in Airam culture. At one point, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] even sought to harness this by using conscripts whom clan leader [[Ilay]] had sold to the Empire as [[Ramming/Legends|suicide strike]] pilots. The Airam later avenged themselves on the Empire by, ironically, using kamikaze attacks to destroy the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Executor-class)|''Vengeance'']].

Starships commonly used by the Airam included [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]], [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]], [[Muurian transport]]s, [[R-41 Starchaser]]s and [[R60 T-wing interceptor|T-wing interceptors]].

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]''
